---
title: "codon_wilcoxon_condition — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall global_occupancy — occupancy stats"
date: 2026-06-04
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [22, 9, 23, 23, 23]  # Stage-1 (raw-p deferred); Stage-2 widens to 6-col [22, 8, 18, 17, 17, 26]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![Three-panel bar plot, A-site / P-site / E-site left to right, of per-codon log2 fold-change (BWM median / control median) in whole-transcriptome occupancy. Within each panel the codons are sorted by log2 fold-change high to low; each bar is one codon, coloured by amino-acid class (acidic, basic, hydrophobic, polar, neutral) with stop codons in grey (see legend). Y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.2 to +0.85; bars above zero are BWM-enriched, below zero are BWM-depleted.](../plots/between_condition/codon/composite/APE_codon_BWM_vs_Control_barplot_composite.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each panel ranks the codons by signed log2 fold-change at one ribosome site (A, P, E): a bar above zero means more BWM occupancy than control at that site, below zero means less. No significance markers are drawn on the bars.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `codon_wilcoxon_condition.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `between_condition_wilcoxon`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `aa_wilcoxon_condition.csv`
- **Test:** Mann-Whitney U (two-sample Wilcoxon rank-sum), BWM vs control
- **Design:** n=6 BWM vs n=6 control replicates (the 6 replicates per condition span day_0/day_5/day_10, pooled as replicates of one condition); two-sided Mann-Whitney U on per-(site, codon) occupancy; BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site is its own family of ~62 codon tests); effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median_BWM / median_control).

### Family-level caveats

- **timepoint-pooled-confound** — n=6 per condition is built by treating the 6 replicates across day_0/day_5/day_10 as replicates (`rep_to_condition` maps all 6 BWM replicates to "BWM" and all 6 control replicates to "control"); if the BWM effect varies across timepoints, pooling timepoints as within-condition noise masks signal. Both members share this design.
- **mw-floor-tight** — the Mann-Whitney two-sided p-floor for n=6 vs n=6 is ~=0.00216; per-site BH families of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon) make FDR<0.05 feasible but tight.
- **n=6-modest-power** — Mann-Whitney with n=6 vs n=6 has modest power; a null result is weakly informative.
- **bh-per-site** — stats were computed per site (A/P/E) and then merged by `merge_global_occupancy_analysis.py`; `p_adj` is BH-corrected within each site's ~20/61 features, not across the merged A/P/E file.

---

## Headline

No codon test is significant at FDR<0.05 (0 of 186), and none clears raw p<0.05 either (min raw p = 0.1797); the minimum adjusted p is 1.0. There is no detectable BWM-vs-control signal at codon resolution. As non-significant leads only: the largest-magnitude cells are the stop codon TGA (`log2_FC` +0.8516 at the A-site and +0.6565 at the E-site, both `p_adj` 1.0000), which carry low footprint counts and most plausibly reflect noise rather than biology; among sense codons the largest cells are E-site GAG (-0.1926), P-site GAG (-0.1768), and A-site TTC (-0.1693).

## Top hits

Effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median BWM / median control): positive means BWM-enriched, negative means BWM-depleted. `p_adj` is the Mann-Whitney p after BH correction within each site's family of ~62 codons (the raw Mann-Whitney p per cell is summarised under Numbers at a glance; min raw p = 0.1797). The `aa` column is the single-letter amino acid translation of each codon (stop codons shown as `*`). Each site is split into one sub-table per direction. No row reaches FDR<0.05 or raw p<0.05, so every row below is a non-significant lead; within each sub-table the rows are the 5 largest-magnitude codons ranked by `|log2_FC|` descending. The only `flag` applied is `low-count` on the stop codon TGA (very low footprint counts make its `log2_FC` unstable); all other cells are blank.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag      |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---                  | ---       |
| TGA   | *  | +0.8516            | 1.0000               | low-count |
| CCG   | P  | +0.2279            | 1.0000               |           |
| CTA   | L  | +0.1246            | 1.0000               |           |
| GGG   | G  | +0.0811            | 1.0000               |           |
| GTC   | V  | +0.0353            | 1.0000               |           |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| TTC   | F  | -0.1693            | 1.0000               |      |
| GAG   | E  | -0.1561            | 1.0000               |      |
| GTG   | V  | -0.1376            | 1.0000               |      |
| GAA   | E  | -0.1343            | 1.0000               |      |
| GAC   | D  | -0.0889            | 1.0000               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| CGG   | R  | +0.1259            | 1.0000               |      |
| TTA   | L  | +0.1207            | 1.0000               |      |
| AGG   | R  | +0.1081            | 1.0000               |      |
| CTG   | L  | +0.0947            | 1.0000               |      |
| GGC   | G  | +0.0620            | 1.0000               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| GAG   | E  | -0.1768            | 1.0000               |      |
| GTG   | V  | -0.1057            | 1.0000               |      |
| AAC   | N  | -0.1019            | 1.0000               |      |
| TGC   | C  | -0.0691            | 1.0000               |      |
| AGC   | S  | -0.0370            | 1.0000               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag      |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---                  | ---       |
| TGA   | *  | +0.6565            | 1.0000               | low-count |
| GTC   | V  | +0.1044            | 1.0000               |           |
| TGC   | C  | +0.0736            | 1.0000               |           |
| TGT   | C  | +0.0735            | 1.0000               |           |
| GCA   | A  | +0.0320            | 1.0000               |           |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| GAG   | E  | -0.1926            | 1.0000               |      |
| AAG   | K  | -0.1407            | 1.0000               |      |
| AGA   | R  | -0.1068            | 1.0000               |      |
| AAC   | N  | -0.0968            | 1.0000               |      |
| CAG   | Q  | -0.0862            | 1.0000               |      |

### Flag glossary

- `low-count` — the codon's footprint count or median frequency is below the test's stability threshold; the effect column for this row is unstable and should not be over-read. Here it marks the stop codon TGA, whose large `log2_FC` (A-site +0.8516, E-site +0.6565) reflects very low counts rather than biology.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 186 (62 per site, A/P/E)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 0
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 0
- `min adjusted-p`: 1.0
- `min raw p`: 0.1797
- `p_floor`: 0.00216 (theoretical two-sided Mann-Whitney floor for n=6 vs n=6). Observed min raw p (0.1797) sits far above this floor, so the null here is signal/power-limited, not floor-limited.
- Per site: every site min `p_adj` = 1.0000.

## Biological interpretation

**There is no detectable BWM-vs-control difference at codon resolution under the pooled-timepoint design (concrete).** None of the 186 (site, codon) tests passes BH within its ~62-codon site family; the minimum adjusted p is 1.0 and no cell clears raw p<0.05 (min raw p 0.1797), so this is not a correction artefact. The codon resolution does not rescue what the amino-acid sister `aa_wilcoxon_condition.csv` could not see — both files are no-signal at FDR for the same BWM-vs-control contrast. This rules out a large, timepoint-stable shift in which codons occupy the A, P, or E site between BWM and control. It does not rule out a timepoint-localised effect: pooling the six replicates across day_0/5/10 turns any day-specific shift into within-condition noise, and at n=6 vs 6 the test has only modest power, so a real day-specific effect could be missed here.

**The largest-magnitude cells are directional leads only, with no value above the no-signal band (exploratory).** The single largest cells are the stop codon TGA at the A-site (`log2_FC` +0.8516) and E-site (+0.6565); both carry very low footprint counts and are most consistent with sampling noise, not biology, so their magnitudes should not be quoted as effects. Among sense codons, the recurring lead is GAG (glutamate): it is the largest or near-largest depletion at all three sites (A -0.1561, P -0.1768, E -0.1926), always at `p_adj` 1.0000. A consistent tri-site direction for one codon is the cleanest exploratory pattern in the file, but with every cell far from significance it is most naturally read as sampling coherence rather than a hit. The other largest sense cells (A-site TTC -0.1693, E-site AAG -0.1407) do not repeat across sites. None of these is a finding; they are directions to check, not conclusions.

**What to carry forward.** Concrete claim to quote: this file shows no significant BWM-vs-control difference at FDR for codon occupancy with timepoints pooled, matching the amino-acid sister. Exploratory directions worth checking in sister tests: (1) does the GAG tri-site depletion reappear with the same direction in the per-timepoint Fisher at codon resolution, where BWM-vs-control power is not diluted by pooling across day_0/5/10? (2) does any amino-acid-level lead from the sister `aa_wilcoxon_condition.csv` localise here to specific synonymous codons, or do synonyms move together? Noise to ignore: the stop codon TGA magnitudes (low-count), and the bulk of cells sitting at `p_adj` 1.0000 with no direction.

## Methods

Columns present: `site`, `codon`, `median_BWM`, `median_control`, `log2_FC`, `U_stat`, `p_value`, `p_adj`. The test is a two-sample Wilcoxon rank-sum (Mann-Whitney U) comparing BWM vs control occupancy per (site, codon). The `U_stat` column and the paired median columns are consistent with that test. The correction column is `p_adj` (BH within each site's ~62-codon family, per `merge_global_occupancy_analysis.py`). The test compares the two condition distributions per cell; it does not address timepoint structure (the 6 replicates per condition span day_0/day_5/day_10) and does not separate synonymous-codon effects from amino-acid-level effects.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **timepoint-pooled-confound.** Pooling the 6 replicates across day_0/5/10 as condition replicates means a real day-localised BWM effect can wash out as within-condition noise; a null here does not imply no effect at every timepoint, so cross-check the per-timepoint Fisher at codon resolution before reading it as biological absence.
- **mw-floor-tight.** The exact Mann-Whitney floor for n=6 vs 6 is ~=0.00216, so per-site BH over ~62 codons is feasible only at the very smallest p in the family; here the data does not approach the floor (min raw p 0.1797), so this constraint is not what produced the null.
- **n=6-modest-power.** With n=6 vs 6, an effect of biologically meaningful size can be missed; treat this null as weakly informative, not strong evidence of equivalence.
- **bh-per-site.** `p_adj` is BH-corrected within each site's ~62-codon family, not across the merged A/P/E file, so compare adjusted p only within a single site.

---

## Plots — individual

![A-site BWM-vs-control codon bar plot. X-axis is the codons ordered by log2 fold-change high to low; y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.2 to +0.85; bars coloured by amino-acid class with stop codons in grey (see legend). The stop codon TGA dominates the left tail (~+0.85); largest sense enrichment at CCG (~+0.23) and CGG (~+0.21); largest depletion at TTC (~-0.17) and GAG (~-0.16).](../plots/between_condition/codon/individual/site_A_codon_BWM_vs_Control_barplot.png)

![P-site BWM-vs-control codon bar plot. X-axis is the codons ordered by log2 fold-change high to low; y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.2 to +0.15; bars coloured by amino-acid class with stop codons in grey. Largest enrichment at CTC (~+0.13) and CGG (~+0.12); largest depletion at GAG (~-0.18); the stop codon TGA is modest at this site (~+0.10).](../plots/between_condition/codon/individual/site_P_codon_BWM_vs_Control_barplot.png)

![E-site BWM-vs-control codon bar plot. X-axis is the codons ordered by log2 fold-change high to low; y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.2 to +0.65; bars coloured by amino-acid class with stop codons in grey. The stop codon TGA dominates the left tail (~+0.65); largest sense enrichment at CGC (~+0.13) and GTC (~+0.10); largest depletion at GAG (~-0.19), GGA (~-0.14), and AAG (~-0.14).](../plots/between_condition/codon/individual/site_E_codon_BWM_vs_Control_barplot.png)
